steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Start with the given equation $b - 17 = \dfrac{x}{y}$. To isolate $x$, multiply both sides by $y$: $y(b - 17) = x$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Distribute $y$ on the left: $x = by - 17y$. This is the expression for $x$ in terms of $b$ and $y$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $x = \dfrac{b-17}{y}$ — divides by $y$ instead of multiplying; substituting gives $\dfrac{(b-17)/y}{y} = \dfrac{b-17}{y^2} \neq b-17$. Eliminated.
  • B: $x = by - 17y$ — equals $y(b-17)$; substituting gives $\dfrac{by-17y}{y} = b-17$. That matches Choice B — $x = by - 17y$.
  • C: $x = \dfrac{by-17}{y}$ — distributes incorrectly; substituting gives $\dfrac{(by-17)/y}{y} = \dfrac{by-17}{y^2}$, not $b-17$. Eliminated.
  • D: $x = by - 17$ — fails to multiply the 17 by $y$; substituting gives $\dfrac{by-17}{y} = b - \dfrac{17}{y} \neq b-17$. Eliminated.
